_meta.purpose: Parts-based framework for character creation and NPC design
_meta.note: IFS views the mind as naturally containing multiple sub-personalities (parts)

core_concept: Everyone has a core Self (calm, curious, compassionate) and multiple Parts that developed to protect them.

exiles.name: Exiles
exiles.role: Carry the pain, shame, fear, and trauma from past experiences.
exiles.behavior: Hold wounded emotions; are kept hidden by Managers and Firefighters.
exiles.game_mapping: The character's backstory wounds — what they don't talk about.
exiles.npc_mapping: An NPC who carries visible vulnerability and needs protection.
exiles.therapeutic_goal: Witness, validate, and unburden these parts safely.
exiles.example_in_game: A quest item that represents something the character lost — retrieving it is therapeutic.

managers.name: Managers
managers.role: Proactive protectors that try to prevent emotional pain before it happens.
managers.behavior: Control, plan, criticize, people-please, achieve, avoid, intellectualize.
managers.game_mapping: The character's defense mechanisms — how they navigate the world.
managers.npc_mapping: An NPC who is overly cautious, controlling, or perfectionistic — relatable to the player.
managers.therapeutic_goal: Appreciate their protective intent while relaxing rigid control.
managers.example_in_game: A quest where the character must let go of control to succeed.

firefighters.name: Firefighters
firefighters.role: Reactive protectors that act urgently when Exiles' pain breaks through.
firefighters.behavior: Distract, numb, rage, binge, use substances, dissociate, self-harm.
firefighters.game_mapping: The character's crisis responses — what they do when overwhelmed.
firefighters.npc_mapping: An NPC who acts impulsively or self-destructively but has good reasons underneath.
firefighters.therapeutic_goal: Understand what they're protecting and find less extreme alternatives.
firefighters.example_in_game: A moment where the character's companion NPC acts out — and the player must understand why, not punish.

character_creation: What quality does your character have that they're most proud of? (Self)
What does your character hide from the world? (Exile)
How does your character protect themselves? (Manager)
What does your character do when things get too much? (Firefighter)
game_integration.antagonist_as_part: The game's antagonist can represent an extreme Protector part — something that was once helpful but became rigid and harmful.
game_integration.healing_arc: The narrative arc mirrors IFS unburdening: recognize the part → understand its intent → witness its pain → offer it a new role.
